subject: Biology
title: Cell Theory: Three Rules of Life
description: A short lesson to help you apply Cell Theory: Three Rules of Life.

hook.text: Before the 1800s, people believed maggots sprang from meat and mice from grain.

buildup.text: Schleiden and Schwann proposed that all plants and animals are made of cells.

discovery.text: Virchow added the key third rule: every cell comes from a pre-existing cell.

twist.text: Pasteur's swan-neck flask experiment killed spontaneous generation for good.

climax.text: Three rules—all life is cells, cells are the basic unit, cells come from cells.

punchline.text: Three rules that explain all living things.

quiz.question: Who added the rule that all cells come from pre-existing cells?
